title: XPeng Volkswagen EV Rollout Puts AI Partnership In Investor Focus
text: XPeng and Volkswagen Group announced entry into a framework agreement for strategic technical collaboration and strategic minority investment by Volkswagen Group in XPeng. The agreement, announced on August 15, 2025, significantly expands their technical collaboration on E/E architecture. Volkswagen Group expanded technical collaboration with XPeng, allowing XPeng's E/E Architecture to be adopted across Volkswagen's full range of platforms, including electric, hybrid, and internal combustion vehicles, in China. Volkswagen earlier made a strategic minority investment in XPENG, acquiring approximately 4.99% of the company's outstanding share capital for around US$700 million. XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) and Volkswagen began mass production of the jointly developed ID. UNYX 08 full-size electric SUV, integrating XPeng's autonomous driving system and Turing AI chips into Volkswagen's EV platform. The ID. UNYX 08 is the first in a broader plan by the partners to roll out more than 20 new models. XPeng announced February 2026 deliveries of 15,256 vehicles and officially launched its second-generation VLA 2.0 autonomous driving system, with Volkswagen as the first external customer.
